1.premise: A Japanese mother-in-law's pancreatic cancer surgery yields absurd achievements and a gut-spilling pun.
Removing a pancreas could be inventing triabetics.

2.premise: "Spilling guts" can mean revealing secrets or exposing internal organs.

lines.text: - Hey, everybody.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Waka waka waka.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Nanu nanu.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: My mother-in-law survived stage four pancreatic cancer
lines.label: setup

lines.text: by having her pancreas cut out.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: (gagging)
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: That's right.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Yeah, my mother-in-law was born in Sendai, Japan,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: And in Japan, they don't have the same diseases that we do.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: They don't have the same health problems.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: They don't have pancreatic cancer.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So they gave her two choices.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: They were like, you can either live and we,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: "Cut out your pancreas," or you keep your pancreas in
lines.label: setup

lines.text: and you die.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So she was like, "I want to leave."
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So they, "Cut out our pancreas."
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So she now has a permanent insulin pump
lines.label: setup

lines.text: and type 3 diabetes, which I like to call triabetics.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: So in one woman, my mother-in-law,
lines.label: tag

lines.text: the Japanese cured pancreatic cancer,
lines.label: tag

lines.text: they invented a cyborg, and they perfected triabetics.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: So you know why these Japanese can't keep a secret?
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Because they're always spilling their guts.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: [GRUNTS]
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: You see that?
lines.label: fluff
